description: A Kanban board web application built with Bun + React + TailwindCSS, featuring SQLite persistence and drag-and-drop task management with a Solarized Dark theme.

user_stories.title: View Default Columns
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to see three default columns (Todo, In Progress, Done) when I open the app, so I can immediately start organizing my tasks.
acceptance_criteria: App loads with three columns by default
Default column names are: 'Todo', 'In Progress', 'Done'
Columns are displayed horizontally on the screen
Each column shows its title and contains any existing tasks

user_stories.title: Create New Task
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to create a new task with a title and description in any column, so I can capture work items.
acceptance_criteria: Each column has an 'Add Task' button
Clicking 'Add Task' opens a form with title and description fields
Task title is required (cannot be empty)
Task description is optional
Task description can render markdown
New task is saved to SQLite database
New task appears immediately in the column without page refresh
Form can be cancelled without creating a task

user_stories.title: Edit Task
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to edit the title and description of an existing task, so I can update task information.
acceptance_criteria: Each task has an edit button/icon
Clicking edit opens a modal with current title and description in editable fields
Changes are saved to SQLite database on submit
Changes are reflected immediately in the UI
Edit form can be cancelled without saving changes
Title validation (cannot be empty) applies to edits

user_stories.title: Delete Task
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to delete a task, so I can remove tasks that are no longer relevant.
acceptance_criteria: Each task has a delete button/icon
Clicking delete shows a confirmation dialog
Confirmed deletion permanently removes the task from SQLite
Task is removed from UI immediately after confirmation
Delete operation can be cancelled from confirmation dialog

user_stories.title: Drag and Drop Tasks
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to drag and drop tasks between columns, so I can easily move tasks through my workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Tasks are draggable with mouse cursor
Draggable visual feedback is shown during drag (e.g., opacity change, shadow)
Drop zones highlight when dragging over a valid column
Dropping a task on a column moves the task to that column
Task movement is persisted to SQLite database
Drag and drop works smoothly without UI lag
Task maintains its ID and other properties when moved

user_stories.title: Rename Column
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to rename a column, so I can customize column names to match my workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Each column header has an edit/rename option
Clicking rename opens an inline edit or modal with current column name
Column name is required (cannot be empty)
Renamed column name is saved to SQLite database
Column title updates immediately in UI
Rename operation can be cancelled

user_stories.title: Add New Column
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to add a new column, so I can create custom workflow stages.
acceptance_criteria: There is an 'Add Column' button visible on the board
Clicking 'Add Column' prompts for a column name
Column name is required (cannot be empty)
New column is saved to SQLite database
New column appears in the board layout
New column is initially empty (no tasks)

user_stories.title: Delete Column
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to delete a column, so I can remove workflow stages that are no longer needed.
acceptance_criteria: Each column header has a delete option
Clicking delete shows a confirmation dialog
Deleting a column also deletes all tasks within that column
Column and associated tasks are removed from SQLite
Column is removed from UI immediately after confirmation
Delete operation can be cancelled from confirmation dialog
Default columns (Todo, In Progress, Done) cannot be deleted

user_stories.title: Reorder Columns
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to reorder columns, so I can arrange my workflow in the desired sequence.
acceptance_criteria: Columns are draggable with mouse cursor
Draggable visual feedback is shown during drag
Drop zones highlight when dragging over a valid column position
Dropping a column reorders the columns in the board
Column order is saved to SQLite database
Reordering is reflected immediately in UI

user_stories.title: View Task Details
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to see the full description of a task, so I can understand the work required.
acceptance_criteria: Task cards display the task title prominently
Task description is visible (either on card or on hover/click)
Long descriptions are handled gracefully (truncation with 'more' option)
Task metadata (created date, moved date) is optionally displayed

user_stories.title: Solarized Dark Theme
user_stories.description: As a user, I want the app to use a Solarized Dark color scheme, so I have a comfortable viewing experience.
acceptance_criteria: App uses Solarized Dark color palette throughout
Base colors: base03 (#002b36), base02 (#073642), base01 (#586e75), base00 (#657b83), base0 (#839496), base1 (#93a1a1), base2 (#eee8d5), base3 (#fdf6e3)
Accent colors: yellow (#b58900), orange (#cb4b16), red (#dc322f), magenta (#d33682), violet (#6c71c4), blue (#268bd2), cyan (#2aa198), green (#859900)
Text has proper contrast ratios for readability
Interactive elements (buttons, links) have clear hover states

user_stories.title: Persist Data to SQLite
user_stories.description: As a user, I want all my board data to be saved to SQLite, so I don't lose my work when I close the app.
acceptance_criteria: All tasks are stored in SQLite database
All columns are stored in SQLite database
Column order is persisted in SQLite
Database file is created automatically on first run
Data persists across app restarts
Database operations handle errors gracefully

user_stories.title: Display Task details in a modal with rendered markdown
user_stories.description: As a user I want to see the full task details in a modal with rendered markdown when I click on a task
acceptance_criteria: Clicking on a task opens a modal window
The task's description is rendered in Markdown
I can still drag & drop task around
